Title: The 13-lipoxygenase GmLOX6 is involved in JA biosynthesis and serves as a positive regulator of salt stress tolerance in soybean

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Lipoxygenase family; 13-LOX type II; contains PLAT domain and lipoxygenase domain
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Octadecanoid pathway; chloroplast initiation step of JA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor for JA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxygenates α-linolenic acid, preferentially over linoleic acid, to generate the 13-hydroperoxide precursor that initiates jasmonic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates plant stress adaptation, including salt tolerance, ROS detoxification, and ion homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Preferentially expressed in roots; induced by NaCl with a peak at 6 h; strongly induced by exogenous JA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Preferentially expressed in roots; induced by NaCl with a peak at 6 h; strongly induced by exogenous JA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 120 mM NaCl salt stress; exogenous JA treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Subcellular localization; recombinant enzyme kinetics assay; soybean overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; JA quantification by UPLC-MS/MS; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; exogenous JA rescue; physiological and ion/ROS assays
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GSA: CRA026857; metabolite quantification of JA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point/initial committed entry step for stress-induced JA biosynthesis in this study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes JA accumulation and enhances soybean salt tolerance by improving growth, photosynthetic performance, ROS scavenging, membrane stability, Na+ extrusion, and K+/Na+ homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmLOX6 encodes a chloroplast-localized 13-LOX with strong preference for α-linolenic acid. 2. Overexpression increases endogenous JA, whereas CRISPR knockout reduces JA under control and salt stress conditions. 3. GmLOX6 positively regulates salt tolerance by enhancing antioxidant capacity, reducing ROS and membrane damage, and improving Na+/K+ balance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for gene editing or overexpression to improve soybean salt tolerance and potentially broader stress resilience through enhanced JA biosynthesis; superior haplotypes and marker-assisted deployment were proposed
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Overexpression also enhanced vegetative growth under normal conditions; knockout reduced growth. Exogenous JA partially/alleviated salt sensitivity in all genotypes, supporting JA-dependent function.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression through direct promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible JA-responsive feedback modulation inferred from biphasic JA-induced expression, but not conclusively established
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes salt stress adaptation through ROS detoxification and ion homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in roots; induced by salt stress with maximum at 24 h; biphasic response to JA with peaks at 3 h and 12 h
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in roots; induced by salt stress with maximum at 24 h; biphasic response to JA with peaks at 3 h and 12 h
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 120 mM NaCl salt stress; JA treatment
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in dual-luciferase effector assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Molecular docking prediction; Y1H; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay; subcellular localization; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout in soybean hairy roots; RT-qPCR; JA quantification
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a negative regulator of JA accumulation and salt tolerance by repressing GmLOX6; knockout increases GmLOX6 expression, JA content, and salt tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct upstream repressor of the focal JA-biosynthetic gene GmLOX6 and thus a negative controller of JA-mediated salt tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmRWP-RK11 directly binds the GmLOX6 promoter at core motifs and represses its transcription. 2. Loss of GmRWP-RK11 elevates GmLOX6 expression and JA accumulation in roots. 3. CRISPR disruption of GmRWP-RK11 enhances soybean salt tolerance, supporting its role as a negative regulator of the GmLOX6-JA axis.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Knockout or weakening of GmRWP-RK11 is a practical strategy to derepress GmLOX6, increase JA biosynthesis, and improve soybean salt tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Binding motifs in the GmLOX6 promoter included M2 (AAAAGGCA) and M3 (CAGCA); Arg-33 of GmRWP-RK11 was predicted to contribute to promoter recognition.
